title: The Stinger
timelinePosition: Opens the night after Chapter 24's verdict, with Jaina and Sylvanas still in adjacent palace cells while Frostmourne remains active in the vault above.
openLocation: Capital City palace dungeons — adjacent cells divided by two feet of stone
transport: Static imprisonment only. Motion belongs to the blade's pulse, voices through the wall, and the visitor who ignores walls entirely.
immediateObjective: Survive the prison night, move the blade intelligence through stone, and hold onto the partnership long enough for it to remain useful.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Scene 1 stays inside Jaina and Scene 2 stays inside Sylvanas
No prison break, release route, or legal reversal yet
Frostmourne remains an active background threat from line one; its seeking pressure cannot fade behind reflection or villain spectacle
Do not turn the visitor into a lore lecture: scale, age, and pattern recognition must land before taxonomy

povKnowsAtOpen: Frostmourne is in the evidence vault above them and its cycle is accelerating.
The verdict changed nothing about the blade's appetite.
Sylvanas is alive on the other side of the wall and still capable of carrying intelligence forward if a surface appears.
Lor'themar is in the city, but the door between knowledge and action is still shut.
Jaina already knows the anti-blade counter; the practical problem is delivery, not theory.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How fast the blade will actually choose a next mind if nothing interrupts it.
Whether any ally outside the cells can move fast enough to matter.
What exactly comes through the dark in Scene 2.
How many earlier hands or worlds Frostmourne has already consumed.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No clean taxonomy for the visitor inside immediate POV; the women understand scale before labels.
No prison break or release route yet.
No assumption that knowing the larger board makes the smaller problem any easier.
No claim that Jaina knows the full five-face pattern before Sylvanas's memory cascade earns it.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Jaina finally names the truth of her arc: being right alone was never enough.
The larger board exists and the enemy behind Arthas is older, broader, and more amused than the women knew.
Frostmourne will seek another hand because that is what it was built to do.
The being visiting the cell has worn five earlier faces across the investigation.
Sylvanas now has a linguistic tell tied to that being's disguises.
Book 2 begins with the pair still imprisoned, the blade seeking, and the new enemy still in the city.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: She has the answer to Frostmourne and nowhere to put it but through a wall.
pov.surfaceObjective: Track the blade's cycle, get the important parts through to Sylvanas, and refuse to let the cell reduce the truth to private despair.
pov.pressureSource: The accelerating heartbeat overhead, the guilt of victory that did not save the dead, the closed door, and the realization that understanding without delivery is just another dead end.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: The last shreds of Jaina's old lie still whisper that if she can think clearly enough, the rest should follow.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can say out loud that the partnership, not solitary intelligence, was the only mechanism that ever worked.
pov.forcedChoice: Spend the night sinking inward, or turn even the wall into one more working channel.
pov.endStateShift: Jaina moves from trapped intelligence to named truth — right together, or not enough.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Book 2 opens with Jaina still imprisoned but no longer misunderstanding what actually solved Book 1.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The second mind on the other side of the wall, then the woman who has to meet the impossible visitor without a weapon or a door that matters.
throughPov.povPressure: Jaina experiences Sylvanas first as a voice still capable of use, then the chapter shifts so Sylvanas becomes the reader who turns impossible language and memory into the sequel's first tool.
throughPov.interactionRule: Keep the wall-talk lean and practical; no sentimental easing of the prison state.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Reduced to voices, breath, and counting through masonry.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The partnership survives full institutional collapse and becomes the thematic answer of the book rather than only its best method.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Let the wall-talk, the visitor's scale, and the final memory cascade carry the chapter. Do not write explanatory speeches where the stone and the silence can do the work.

entries.rule: Present through pulse, cold, and predation. Never reduce Frostmourne to background scenery.
Off-page pressure only. Their presence matters because the door is still closed.
Immediate POV does not need the name first. The horror is scale, ease, and oldness.
The phrase must land as impossible before it lands as pattern-key.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Book 2 opens from this prison state — Frostmourne seeking, the Nathrezim thread active, and the partnership still the only mechanism that actually works.

scenes.title: Through the Wall
scenes.sceneFunction: Let Jaina name both the blade's urgency and her own hard-won truth before the larger enemy arrives.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Verdict delivered, cells closed, blade active.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep darkness, stone, pulse-counting, and voice through masonry concrete so the scene stays intimate and material.

enrichment.detail: Capital City's palace sublevels should feel like ceremonial power turned hostile underground — stone meant to hold evidence now carrying wrong cold from a vault that was never built for a weapon like Frostmourne. The blade's unheld state works through pulse, whispers, and temperature-drop first, so Jaina's awareness should feel like hostile rhythm through stone rather than a clean magical briefing.

beats.dilemma: Jaina can hear exactly what the blade is doing and still cannot put a hand on the door that would let her stop it.
beats.decision: She keeps counting instead of letting the pulse become one more form of helpless noise.
beats.valueShift: condemned -> the blade becomes the real night
newOnPageInformation: The cycle is shortening from pulse to pulse.
The blade is not broadcasting blindly; it is narrowing toward individual minds.
Jaina can now distinguish the difference between ambient hunger and targeted interest.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Which mind the blade lands on first.
Whether the palace wards fail before a person does.
sensoryAnchors: wrong heartbeat through stone above her
guard-mind brushing the blade's radius and catching on it
bread-taste and pulse becoming the same rhythm in her body
beats.transitionIntent: truth-pressure: once the blade is understood as the active threat, the chapter can force Jaina to say what Book 1 actually taught her.
beats.verifierNotes: Adjacent cells and blade-proximate imprisonment follow the chapter24 verdict aftermath; do not imply random prison placement or a neutral room reset.

beats.goal: Speak the truth of what stopped Arthas.
beats.conflict: The truth humiliates Jaina's old self-concept because it says seeing clearly was never enough by itself.

outcome.summary: Jaina says it aloud and Sylvanas hears it, but the truth arrives in a cell where the pair can barely act on it beyond continuing to think together.

beats.valueShift: the blade becomes the real night -> the book's truth is named
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can now name border work, grain work, Scholomance, Northrend, and the throne room as partnership achievements rather than solitary insights.
Kel'Thuzad becomes the warning version of solitary brilliance rather than merely the enemy teacher.
Sylvanas answers from the wall not by soothing Jaina but by sharpening the statement into something usable.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How this truth changes the next investigation once the door actually opens.
Whether the world outside the cells ever learns the same lesson.
sensoryAnchors: consonants blunted by two feet of stone
long pauses that still feel full because the other voice is listening
one sentence crossing the wall harder than the verdict did
beats.transitionIntent: practical-pressure: once the truth is spoken, Jaina can turn immediately back to the delivery problem.

beats.dilemma: Jaina knows how to hurt the blade and still cannot touch it.
beats.decision: She starts packaging the intelligence for Sylvanas and for anyone outside who might yet become a delivery surface.
beats.valueShift: the book's truth is named -> partnership becomes working channel again
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can already describe the next likely vulnerable targets in practical terms.
The counter remains valid even outside the throne room, but only if delivered in time.
The partnership's wall-language is now sufficient to carry technical threat reporting.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether outside allies can receive and act on the intelligence before the blade chooses.
Whether the next wielder is in the palace already or only being shaped toward it.
sensoryAnchors: fingertip tapping marking cycle length between spoken lines
stone making every sentence cost extra effort
the blade spiking harder every time Jaina says something true about it
beats.transitionIntent: scale-widening: once the counter is moving through the wall, the chapter can widen the board by letting an older cold violate the same stone.
beats.verifierNotes: The wall-passed counter grows out of the existing days-not-weeks blade clock; do not stage this as a fresh discovery problem.

scenes.title: The Visitor
scenes.sceneFunction: Reframe the scale of the story, confirm that the blade is still in play, and resolve the five-face thread into a sequel-hunt.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The partnership still works through stone and the blade is seeking above them.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep torch behavior, cold shift, stone-wall violation, and the ancient phrase concrete so the scene lands through sensory impossibility.

enrichment.detail: Mal'Ganis should read like an immortal infiltrator finally dropping the polite mask, not like a new battlefield monster introduced from nowhere: a Nathrezim with maker-level familiarity around plague infrastructure and Frostmourne's wielder-cycle. That makes the line about another hand feel procedural and old, while the ancient star-road farewell lands as the one habit he never bothered to cull.

beats.emotion: Vigilance hitting the edge of a category Sylvanas does not yet possess.
beats.dilemma: She has no weapon and no useful classification, only the choice to face what is coming or cower from it.
beats.decision: She stands and faces it because anything else would make no tactical sense even if tactics are now failing.
beats.valueShift: partnership as working channel -> the room stops belonging to known threats
newOnPageInformation: The blade's cold is not the only cold in the prison.
Jaina's fear through the wall is qualitatively different from everything before it.
The darkness itself changes before the visitor fully appears.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the blade and the visitor are allied, parallel, or nested.
sensoryAnchors: torch flame shrinking inward
air gaining weight before shape
Jaina's sharp inhale through the wall
beats.transitionIntent: revelation-pressure: once the room belongs to the unknown, the chapter can let the visitor speak and show what scale it operates on.
beats.verifierNotes: The visitor arrives against already-established vault-cold and prison acoustics; keep the second cold as escalation, not first-contact exposition for the setting.

beats.goal: Understand what the visitor thinks the women actually accomplished.
beats.conflict: The visitor does not deny their victory; it simply places that victory on a board so large it almost erases the satisfaction of it.

outcome.summary: The pair learn the board is vastly larger and that Arthas was only one piece, and the visitor casually confirms the blade is designed to seek again.

newOnPageInformation: Arthas's death is a setback to the larger enemy, not a decisive blow.
The plague, Cult, and Scourge apparatus already accomplished more than the pair understood while they were stopping the prince.
The line about the blade finding another hand lands with maker-level familiarity rather than rumor-level fear.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Exactly where this being sits relative to the Lich King and the plague apparatus.
Who the next hand might be.
sensoryAnchors: voice too calm for what it is saying
scale conveyed not by explanation but by boredom
Jaina's steadying breath through the wall as she hears the blade line land
beats.transitionIntent: departure-pressure: once the blade's future is confirmed, the chapter can make the visitor's leaving more frightening than its arrival.

beats.goal: Survive the visitor's departure and catch the one impossible phrase that turns memory into pattern.
beats.conflict: The visitor ignores stone, warding, and prison architecture as if those things only ever applied to smaller beings.

outcome.summary: The visitor leaves untouched and unopposed, and its final farewell in ancient Kaldorei provides the missing key Sylvanas needed to place every earlier encounter.

newOnPageInformation: The visitor never treated the cell as confinement in the first place.
The original farewell is older than any living polity in the current world.
The repeated Common phrase from earlier chapters was always an echo of something much older.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the visitor intended the clue as mockery, sloppiness, or deliberate bait.
How much of the investigation it steered from behind those other faces.
sensoryAnchors: body passing through solid stone without noise or strain
one impossible phrase hanging in the cell after the shape is gone
torch relighting only after the room belongs to ordinary dark again
beats.transitionIntent: pattern-lock: once the original phrase lands, the chapter can let Sylvanas's memory do the rest.

beats.emotion: Pattern-recognition shock — not confusion, but instant alignment.
beats.dilemma: Sylvanas cannot act from this cell, but she also cannot unknow that the same being has been beside the investigation from almost the beginning.
beats.decision: Hold the pattern, the phrase, and the linguistic tell as the first usable counterintelligence filter the partnership can pass outward even from this cell.
newOnPageInformation: The grain trader, the Andorhal contact, the retreating figure, the military aide, and the Stratholme-road merchant all collapse into one operative line.
The repeated star-road farewell is no longer a curiosity; it is a filter for separating one operative's disguises from ordinary witnesses.
The new investigation lane is not just the blade but the disguised manipulator moving through the kingdom behind it, and the clue is transferable beyond the cell.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How many other faces the same being wore that the pair never caught.
Whether the tell remains usable now that the being knows it has been noticed.
sensoryAnchors: five remembered farewells striking in sequence behind the ancient original
one dead language making five old scenes relight at once
Frostmourne pulsing above them while the pattern locks into place below
beats.transitionIntent: handoff-pressure: Book 2 opens from this locked state — prison, blade, demon, and a now-usable farewell tell the partnership can push outward through the wall.
beats.verifierNotes: This is recognition of the long-running farewell thread, not a brand-new clue; do not reintroduce the five earlier faces as fresh evidence.

warnings: Beats 4-6 run a monotonic downward line from working-channel competence into scale shock and impossible recognition. Differentiate unknown-presence dread, blade-cycle confirmation, and language-trigger revelation sharply so the descent does not flatten.

Late scene-beat outcomes skew negative: beat 2 is the lone yes-but, while beats 5 and 6 both land no-and. Make beat 7's pattern-lock feel like a real usable gain or the chapter will read as pure collapse.

The Scene 1 -> Scene 2 POV handoff is load-bearing. Keep both lanes absolute: Jaina names the book's truth before the visitor arrives, and Sylvanas earns the pattern recognition only after the ancient phrase lands.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter24's locked verdict: the same adjacent-cell imprisonment continues into the first full night after sentencing, with Lor'themar's arrival and outside corroboration still unable to open the door while Jaina keeps hearing Frostmourne through the palace stone as a days-not-weeks clock. Chapter25 opens after the corridor contact has gone quiet and the partnership has been reduced to breath, tapping, and wall-passed analysis — just in time for the larger board to step into that same prison dark.

craftResearch.findings: Keep prison scenes active through sensory confinement, subtext-heavy dialogue, and an off-page clock that keeps pressing even when bodies cannot move.
craftResearch.source: Web search synthesis of prison-scene craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: A stinger should close the current emotional argument while opening a larger unanswered threat, ideally with one concrete gain the next book can act on.
craftResearch.source: Web search synthesis of chapter-ending hook guidance

craftResearch.findings: Let the reveal work through atmosphere, conflict, and implication; one precise operational disclosure lands better than a lore lecture.
craftResearch.source: Web search synthesis of antagonist-reveal craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: Each POV must own a distinct function, the handoff must carry live pressure, and the second POV must add an indispensable new angle rather than repeat the first.
craftResearch.source: Web search synthesis of dual-POV pacing guidance

pacing.notes: Beat 1 opens in active confinement rather than recap, beats 1-3 keep the prison half moving through clock, truth, and delivery problem, and beats 4-6 escalate by changing the source of dread from known blade pressure to unknown visitor to impossible departure. Beat 7 now lands as a usable gain, which prevents the back half from reading as pure collapse.

structure.notes: The chapter has a clean 3/4 split: Jaina names the book-truth and retools the partnership under captivity, then Sylvanas receives the scale reveal and converts it into a sequel hunt lane. The opening is immediate and bodily, the midchapter turn lands at the scene break when the older cold enters the room, and the close survives because the chapter ends on leverage rather than helpless knowledge.

character.notes: POV ownership stays clean: Jaina earns the thematic naming beat, Sylvanas earns the recognition beat, and neither scene requires omniscient leakage. Agency remains intact in every beat through listening, naming, repackaging, standing, questioning, surviving, and pattern-locking. Supporting functions stay distinct: Jaina is epistemic correction, Sylvanas is tactical recognition, and the visitor is scale-expanding pressure rather than a speech machine.

transitions.notes: All 7 beat-level transitionIntent links were checked for causal coverage and clean landing. The chapter now has an explicit chapterBridge from chapter24, beat 3 better telegraphs the cross-scene hard cut by linking the wall-pass to the older cold entering the same stone, and beat 7 now hands Book 2 a transferable farewell tell instead of private consolation.

continuity.notes: 2 new beats, 0 callbacks, and 5 evolutions: most of chapter25 grows directly out of the prison-state, active blade, and established partnership while only the visitor-scale disclosure and impossible departure are genuinely new. No contradictions were found in prison placement, Frostmourne pressure, Jaina and Sylvanass post-verdict state, or the recurring farewell thread; anti-reintroduction guards were added for the established prison cold and the long-running five-face clue.

modifications.description: Annotated every beat with continuityStatus, added beat-level memoryRef entries where the chapter is carrying forward prison-state, partnership, blade-countdown, palace setting, and farewell-thread material, and assembled the downstream requiredMemory reading list.
Added a direct bridge from chapter24s verdict aftermath into the first prison night, clarifying that Lorthemars arrival changes corroboration but not the locked cells before the visitor enters.

Tightened the Scene 1 -> Scene 2 carry and the final sequel so the five-face recognition reads as a usable counterintelligence filter the partnership can push outward from captivity, not just private realization.
Added verifierNotes to prevent prison-setting reset, fresh-discovery framing around the blade clock, and reintroduction of the long-running farewell pattern as brand-new evidence.
